summary: Product owner explicitly selected DEFER_MANUAL_NODE_BATTLE for M5-MANUAL-NODE-BATTLE-DECISION-001. This REVIEW handoff records the decision, keeps manual node battle out of M5 and the 1.0 mainline, prevents implementation or blocking spike tasks for M5-M8, preserves automatic/abstract campaign battle systems, reconciles the status-audit ledger notes, and routes the process-only change for independent systems and QA review. No product code, package code, content, gate document, roadmap, dependency, or battle implementation changed.

tests_run.command: git fetch origin --prune; git checkout main; git pull --ff-only origin main
tests_run.result: main was already synchronized with origin/main at 118edeac00ee4bac365abf2199ec5470bb2d00cd before creating the decision branch.
Task schema validation passed for the manual node battle decision task after recording REVIEW state.
M5 Exit Human Gate task schema validation passed; it remains DRAFT until this decision is integrated and closed.
M5-MANUAL-NODE-BATTLE-DECISION-001 is REVIEW; M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 is DRAFT; prior M0-M4 and completed M5 implementation/evidence tasks remain CLOSED.
No task is dependency-ready while the manual node battle decision is in REVIEW.

Parsed project/goal-mode-state.json, project/messages/outbox/GOAL-MODE-CONTINUATION.json, both active M5 task files, project/tasks/thread-registry.json, and project/model-routing-state.json successfully.
No whitespace errors in the process-only decision diff.

decisions: Manual node battle is not part of M5 and is not part of the 1.0 mainline.
No M5/M6/M7/M8 implementation task, blocking spike, or blocking research task is authorized for manual node battle.
Manual node battle may be recorded only as a post-1.0 expansion or future research candidate; that record is not implementation authorization.
Automatic/abstract battle, campaign preparation, march, supply, siege, postwar governance, and campaign AI systems remain preserved.
After this decision is accepted and integrated, M5-EXIT-HUMAN-GATE-001 becomes the next gate task; M5 passage remains separate and evidence-based.
risks: The previous STATUS_AUDIT document correctly reported a Human Gate blocker before this product-owner decision; this branch adds a follow-up resolution section rather than erasing the historical audit result.
M4 GATE-M1 through GATE-M4 documents remain historical evidence docs and do not necessarily contain the final closure PRs; this is documented as ledger context, not retroactive gate mutation.
taskctl.mjs validate still accepts one task/path at a time; earlier no-argument validation expectations remain a tooling limitation unless a future process task changes taskctl.
requested_action: Review the process-only decision branch. ACCEPT only if the explicit product-owner decision is recorded, manual node battle remains deferred from M5 and the 1.0 mainline, no product or battle implementation changed, status/goal-mode/task files are consistent, and M5 Exit is correctly left as the next unstarted gate. Otherwise REQUEST_CHANGES with exact files and fields.
